original_hypothesis.title: Phantom operator fee accrual via harvestAndDelegateRewards enables systematic depositor principal drain when fees are fetched
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: LiquidRon.harvestAndDelegateRewards credits operatorFeeAmount based on the value returned from proxy.harvestAndDelegateRewards, but in that path rewards are re-delegated rather than transferred into the vault as WRON. This creates a growing fee liability not backed by vault assets; when fetchOperatorFee is called, WRON is withdrawn from the vault balance (user principal) to pay the feeRecipient.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a code-level solvency/accounting bug with direct value transfer. Realism is LOW only because calling harvestAndDelegateRewards and fetchOperatorFee is privileged (owner due to onlyOperator bug, or operator if fixed). It is still critical to validate because it can occur during routine operations.

proof_of_signal.lines: harvestAndDelegateRewards(): operatorFeeAmount += ...; fetchOperatorFee(): withdraws WRON from vault to feeRecipient
proof_of_signal.pattern: Fee accrual without corresponding asset inflow to vault

preconditions: A privileged caller executes harvestAndDelegateRewards at least once
feeRecipient executes fetchOperatorFee before sufficient liquid rewards are realized into the vault

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Fee accrues during auto-compounding even though no WRON enters the vault from that action
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time compounding is used instead of harvesting-to-vault

operational_questions: Does IRoninValidator.delegateRewards return an amount that represents actual rewards moved (and is it ever transferred to the proxy/vault)?
How frequently is harvestAndDelegateRewards expected to run operationally?
Is operatorFeeAmount intended to be paid from principal or from realized rewards only?
validation_requirements: Fork test: call harvestAndDelegateRewards and verify vault WRON balance does not increase while operatorFeeAmount does
Simulate subsequent fetchOperatorFee and quantify depositor principal reduction
Determine if accounting can be corrected by realizing rewards before fee withdrawal

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Privileged actor repeatedly calls harvestAndDelegateRewards to maximize operatorFeeAmount
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Fee recipient calls fetchOperatorFee to withdraw fees from vault WRON
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Depositors experience loss of backing / reduced redeem value
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Repeat to drain significant TVL over time
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Privileged caller and feeRecipient (may be same entity)

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Partial (feeRecipient can extract depositor principal indirectly)
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Solvency erosion; inability for users to withdraw if liquid WRON depleted

execution_requirements_analysis.coordination_complexity: Low (single admin can do both if feeRecipient controlled)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: operatorFeeAmount increasing without corresponding WRON inflows; repeated harvestAndDelegateRewards
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Fee withdrawals exceeding realized rewards; vault WRON balance shrinking during compounding periods
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Fee recipient withdraws shortly after compounding calls

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
